name: beaker-common

metadata.name: beaker-common
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v2 or later (GPLv2+)

documentation: Common components for Beaker packages

modules.name: bkr.common
documentation: Since bkr is a namespace package (and thus cannot have version specific
code in bkr.__init__), the version details are retrieved from here in
order to correctly handle module shadowing on sys.path

Creates the given directory (and any parents), but succeeds if it already
exists.

functions.name: bkr.common.helpers.unlink_ignore
documentation: Unlinks the given path, but succeeds if it doesn't exist.

functions.name: bkr.common.helpers.total_seconds
documentation:
Returns the total number of seconds (float)
represented by the given timedelta.
This is a method on timedelta in Python 2.7+

classes.name: bkr.common.helpers.AtomicFileReplacement

methods.name: bkr.common.helpers.AtomicFileReplacement.create_temp
documentation: Create the temporary file that may later be renamed

methods.name: bkr.common.helpers.AtomicFileReplacement.destroy_temp
documentation: Ensure the temporary file (if any) is destroyed

methods.name: bkr.common.helpers.AtomicFileReplacement.replace_dest
documentation: Move the temporary file to its final destination

Replace a file atomically

Easiest usage is as a context manager, but create_temp, destroy_temp
and replace_dest can also be called directly if needed
Would be nice if Python did this for us: http://bugs.python.org/issue8604

classes.name: bkr.common.helpers.Flock
documentation: Context manager which locks the given path using flock(2).

modules.name: bkr.common.pyconfig
documentation:
Python-like syntax config parser.

USAGE:
    settings = PyConfigParser()
    settings.load_from_{conf, dict, file, string}(...)
    print settings[var]

CONFIG FILE SYNTAX:
PyConfigParser accepts following python-like syntax:
 - variable = <str, int, float, dict, list, tuple>
 - variable = <other_variable>
 - formatting strings can be used:
   - variable = "%s %s" % (var1, var2)
   - variable = "%(key1)s %(key2)s" % <dict>
 - imports are supported:
   - from <file_without_suffix> import *
   - from <file_without_suffix> import var1, var2
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-
This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify
it under the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by
the Free Software Foundation; either version 2 of the License, or
(at your option) any later version.
Copy and pasted from kobo.conf, from kobo 0.4.2-1

functions.name: bkr.common.pyconfig.get_dict_value
documentation:
Return a value from a dictionary, if not found,
use 'glob' keys (*, ? metachars), then use default value with '*' key.

documentation: Program is improperly configured.

*args are tuples (name, value), (name, )

documentation: Return a NUMBER token value.

methods.name: bkr.common.pyconfig.PyConfigParser._get_value
documentation: Get a value (number, string, other variable value, ...).

methods.name: bkr.common.pyconfig.PyConfigParser._get_from_import

Parse 'from <config> import <variables/*>'

documentation: Get a tuple content.
Python-like syntax config parser.

Use this class decorator on a Transport
to retry requests which failed on socket errors.

documentation: Pass response headers to cookie jar.
Fake response class for cookie extraction.

documentation: Return dict with appropriate proxy settings

documentation: Add cookies to the header.

documentation: Kerberos auth - create a client request string

methods.name: bkr.common.xmlrpc3.CookieTransport._kerberos_verify_response
documentation: Kerberos auth - verify client identity

Cookie enabled XML-RPC transport.

USAGE:
\>>> import xmlrpc.client
\>>> from bkr.common.xmlrpc import CookieTransport
\>>> client = xmlrpc.client.ServerProxy("<http:/>/\<server>/xmlrpc", transport=CookieTransport())

For <https://> connections use SafeCookieTransport() instead.

classes.name: bkr.common.xmlrpc3.SafeCookieTransport
documentation:
Cookie enabled XML-RPC transport over HTTPS.

USAGE: see CookieTransport

functions.name: bkr.log.log_to_stream
documentation:
Configures the logging module to send messages to the given stream (for
example, sys.stderr). By default only WARNING and ERROR level messages are
logged; pass the level argument to override this.

Suitable for use in command-line programs.

classes.name: bkr.log.SysLogHandler
documentation: Like logging.handlers.SysLogHandler, but uses libc syslog(3) and splits
multi-line messages into separate log entries.

methods.name: bkr.common.hub.HubProxy._login
documentation:
Login to the hub.
- self._hub instance is created in this method
- session information is stored in a cookie in self._transport

documentation: Login using OAuth2 access token.

methods.name: bkr.common.hub.HubProxy._login_krbv
documentation: Login using kerberos credentials (uses python-gssapi).
A Hub client (thin ServerProxy wrapper).
